primary_text:
Last Tuesday a farmer in Kempsey pulled beef from the cool room. By Thursday it was at the Brookvale hub.

No warehouse. No distribution centre. No weeks in cold storage.

Just 100% grass-fed beef, raised on open pasture. No hormones, no feedlots. The farm name is on every cut.

The colour is different. Deeper red, not pale pink. The fat is golden. It smells clean when it hits the pan.

Once you cook with beef this fresh, the supermarket version stops making sense.
headline: Paddock on Tuesday, yours by Thursday
description: 100% grass-fed beef from named NSW farms. Collected fresh from the Brookvale hub.
creative_brief: Close-up of deep red, marbled grass-fed steak on a timber board. Golden fat visible. Natural kitchen light. No props. Let the colour tell the story.
